privileged_info_summary: The docker_container_forensics task sets up three intentionally misconfigured production containers. acme-webapp (nginx:1.24-alpine) runs with excessive Linux capabilities (SYS_ADMIN, NET_ADMIN, SYS_PTRACE) that are not required for a web server. acme-gateway (python:3.11-slim) contains hardcoded production secrets in environment variables including database credentials, AWS keys, Stripe API keys, and JWT tokens. acme-monitor (alpine:3.18) has a dangerous bind mount of the host /etc directory (mounted read-only to /host-etc), exposing all host configuration to the container. The task requires agents to identify these security issues, create fixed replacement containers, stop the originals, and write an incident report. The agent must use docker inspect, docker exec, and docker logs to discover issues. Login credentials are user 'ga' with password 'password123'. Report must be written to ~/Desktop/incident_report.txt. Pass threshold is 60 points out of 100.
